rulings.Case Title: ALFREDO CHAVEZ VS. ASBESTPS DEFTS(B/P) AS RELECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Tuesday, February 9, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Plaintiff Alfredo Chavez's motion to tax costs is granted in part and denied in part. The following costs are taxed/stricken from the Volkswagen's costs memorandum: 1) $14.26 for lunches during plaintiff's deposition and 2) $8,879.39 for expert fees for Dr. Smith. Meals during depositions are not recoverable. (Ladas v. California State Auto. Assn. (1995) 19 Cal.App.4th 761, 774-775). Dr. Smith's declaration was not reasonably necessary as Tex. Civ. Prac. and Rem. Code §90.007 provides that a defendant's remedy for a plaintiff's failure to serve a report compliant with Tex. Civ. Prac. and Rem. Code §90.003 is to move for dismissal within 30 days of the date such report was required. Volkswagen's CCP 998 offer was reasonable and made in good faith. Charles Redinger's declaration was reasonably necessary for Volkswagan's defense. The $190.00 for personal service of documents was also reasonably necessary for Volkswagan's defense. =(220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: ALBERT LAWRENCE VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS(B/P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Tuesday, February 9, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 2. Defendant Cummins Engine Company's motion for summary judgment is granted. Cummins shifted the burden on the issue of whether the gaskets plaintiff worked with contained asbestos by presenting plaintiff's factually devoid discovery responses on the issue of asbestos content. Plaintiff failed to raise a triable issue regarding asbestos content. Cummins' acknowledgment that "some" of the gaskets contained asbestos is insufficient to permit a trier of fact to conclude that plaintiff will be able to prevail on this issue. Charles Ay's declaration lacks sufficient foundation for his opinion that the gaskets attributable to Cummins more likely than not contained asbestos. The declaration does not explain how Mr. Ay's experience with gaskets from his career as an asbestos insulator gives him a basis for concluding that the gaskets used in diesel engines on the Carolyn S. contained asbestos. Cummins' foundation objection to that declaration is sustained. =(220/HEK)
